evals.prompt: Write me a new PDP headline for my collagen powder. Current headline: 'Premium Hydrolyzed Marine Collagen Peptides Complex.'
evals.expected_output: Three variants of the H1, each following outcome-over-feature framing with specificity premium applied. Should mine customer language before drafting if review excerpts aren't supplied. Each variant should pair with a one-line explanation of structure. Provides recommended test pair. Should not deliver one variant.
assertions: Returns three variants of the headline, not one
Applies outcome-over-feature framing in at least two variants
Uses specificity (numbers, names, dates) in the variants
Names the structure (AIDA, PAS, BAB, or none) per variant
Asks for or mines customer-language inputs
Recommends a test pair between variants

evals.prompt: Our welcome email subject line is 'Welcome to our family!' Open rate is 18%. Help.
evals.expected_output: Diagnoses the subject line as generic and failing specificity, customer-language, and share filters. Offers three replacement variants using specificity premium (numbers, names, outcomes). Asks about audience source (where the signup came from — paid ad versus organic) before final recommendations. References email-marketing for broader subject-line testing methodology.
assertions: Identifies the current subject line as failing the specificity filter
Offers three replacement variants
Each variant uses specificity (numbers or concrete language)
Asks about audience source before final recommendations
References email-marketing for broader testing methodology

evals.prompt: I'm writing a Meta ad for our new fitness apparel line targeting cold audiences. Help me pick a copy structure.
evals.expected_output: Recommends AIDA for cold audiences with no problem awareness, OR PAS if the apparel solves a known fit pain. Walks through which fits when. References ad-creative for the visual-pairing question. Should NOT default to PAS for every product.
assertions: Distinguishes AIDA vs PAS vs BAB based on audience state
Recommends AIDA for cold, unaware audience by default
Notes PAS fits problem-aware audiences (fit pain in apparel)
References ad-creative for visual pairing
Does not default to one structure for all situations

evals.prompt: Our CTA on the PDP says 'Add to Cart.' Conversion is below benchmark. Should we change it?
evals.expected_output: Recommends testing outcome-bearing CTA variants per the action+outcome framework. Provides 3-4 variants like 'Start my 30-day reset,' 'Get my first jar,' 'Send me the daily greens.' Notes first-person framing and concrete outcomes outperform 'Add to Cart' for cold traffic. References ab-testing for the test setup. Notes that 'Add to Cart' may be fine for warm traffic.
assertions: Recommends outcome-bearing CTA variants over generic 'Add to Cart'
Provides at least 3 concrete CTA variants
Notes first-person framing often wins
Distinguishes cold vs warm traffic for CTA choice
References ab-testing for test setup
Output is structured with variants and explanation

evals.prompt: Audit this product description: 'Our innovative, premium daily greens powder uses cutting-edge bioavailability technology and a comprehensive blend of 47 superfoods for maximum health benefits.'
evals.expected_output: Audit flags the description as failing all three filters: specificity (no numbers other than 47, no outcomes), customer-language (inside-the-company words: innovative, premium, cutting-edge, comprehensive, maximum), and share filter (this paragraph could appear on any competitor). Provides a rewrite using outcome-over-feature framing and mined customer language. Notes that customer reviews would inform a better rewrite.
assertions: Flags inside-the-company language (innovative, premium, cutting-edge, comprehensive, maximum)
Notes the description fails the specificity filter
Notes the description fails the share filter (could appear on any competitor)
Provides a rewrite using outcome-over-feature framing
Asks for or references customer reviews as input for a better rewrite
Walks the three filters explicitly

evals.prompt: Write three taglines for my skincare brand. The product is a vitamin C serum.
evals.expected_output: Three taglines using different structures (likely outcome-led, specificity-premium, or BAB). Each tagged with structure and rationale. Asks about ICP and competitor taglines before drafting to avoid me-too output. Notes that taglines benefit from review-mined phrases. Should not deliver one tagline.
assertions: Returns three tagline variants
Tags each with structure or rationale
Asks about ICP or competitor positioning before drafting
References product-marketing-positioning for upstream positioning work
Each variant applies at least one of the frameworks

evals.prompt: My welcome flow email body says: 'Thank you for joining our community! We're so excited to have you. Stay tuned for great content!' Help.
evals.expected_output: Audit identifies the body as failing the share filter and lacking specificity. Recommends rewriting to deliver on a specific promise (whatever the welcome offer is — discount, free shipping, founder note). Walks the headline-promise / body-delivers framework. Provides a rewrite. References email-marketing for the broader welcome flow structure.
assertions: Identifies the body as failing the share filter
Walks the headline-promise / body-delivers framework
Provides a rewrite with specific promise and proof
References email-marketing for broader welcome flow
Asks what the welcome offer or promise actually is
